rows.notes: Top-of-page title. Ink and letters are clear and fully legible; no monetary columns present.
Place name centered under the title. Handwriting is clear and unambiguous.

rows.description: Willm Taylor wife Con. R. to L Day last
rows.notes: Pounds '3' and shillings '0' align clearly in the pound/shilling columns. Pence reads as '5' with a half‑penny symbol (interpreted as 0.5). Description handwriting is mostly clear though abbreviated ('Con. R.' and 'L Day') so slight uncertainty in exact punctuation/spacing.
rows.english_description: Rent payment from William Taylor (and his wife), due at Lady Day.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Rent payment from William Taylor (and his wife) for Lady Day last, amounting to £3 0s 5½d.

rows.description: Widow Ryman ½ year to L Day
rows.notes: Shillings '13' is clear and within valid range. Pence reads as '0' with a half‑penny mark (0.5). The surname appears as 'Ryman' in the handwriting; the half‑year marker '½' is clear.
rows.english_description: Half-year rent payment from Widow Ryman due at Lady Day.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Half-year rent payment from Widow Ryman due at Lady Day, amounting to £0 13s 0½d.

rows.notes: Subtotal line for the 'Little Tew' entries. Numbers '3.13.6' align with the three vertical columns; pence is whole 6 (no fractional mark). Clear underline indicating subtotal.

The descriptive word begins 'Arrear...' (Latin form) — spelling uncertain in one letter but readable. Amounts '308' pounds, '13' shillings are well aligned. Pence reads as '6' with a three‑quarters mark (interpreted as 0.75).

rows.english_description_with_amount: Arrears received for Little Tew, amounting to £308 13s 6¾d.

rows.notes: This is an indented line under the brace with no left description on the page. Amounts '15.2.2' are clear and aligned under the same £/s/d columns.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Unclear — unspecified receipt for Little Tew amounting to £15 2s 2d.

rows.notes: This line is the subtotal label (latin) and is written with an enclosing brace on the page. Amount '327.10.4¼' reads as 327 pounds, 10 shillings, 4 and a quarter pence (¼ as farthing). The Latin spelling 'praesentis'/'praesentis' is slightly variable in the handwriting but context makes it a subtotal.
A repeat of the small local subtotal (3.13.6) appears here as an added entry; digits are clear and align with the columns. No left description present on the line.

rows.notes: Label 'Recepta varia' (various receipts) is legible though hyphenation present. Amount reads '837.3.8¼' — pence shows a farthing mark interpreted as 0.25. Columns are well aligned.
Description 'Debita anni precedentis' (debts of previous year) is readable. Amount '83.2.8¾' shows a three‑quarters symbol after the pence which is read as 0.75. Alignment with columns is clear.
